title: hemerocallis citrina var. vespertina
intro: Hemerocallis citrina var. vespertina, commonly known as the Golden Daylily, is a perennial plant species native to China, characterized by its golden-yellow flowers and sword-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of the Golden Daylily include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its flowers can also be dried and used as a natural food coloring.
